what_it_does: This file is a structured, long-form design-substrate ledger for a 49-page investor pitch deck (slides 1–17, the 'master grammar source'). It documents the narrative arc, per-slide attention flow, and a taxonomy of visual-design findings with recipes, evidence, emergent flags, and generator rules.
observations: The deck is described as a single continuous grammatical sentence formed by slide titles p02–p17, stitched with leading connectives (By / With / Which / But / To / That / And).
Every content slide (p02–p17) shares a faint blueprint/topographic watermark texture at roughly 5–7% opacity over a warm off-white base.
Findings are organized into categories: gradient-shading-opacity, geometry, type-hierarchy, padding-alignment, color-palette, atom, line-connector, texture, chrome-mode, and zone.
Each finding includes a recipe, evidence slide list, emergent boolean, and a generator_rule intended to drive reproduction.
Specific shapes carry semantic meaning: hexagon = ConceptV / Property Wizard system; octagon = Virtual Tours product node; rounded rectangle = process step; circle = stakeholder node.
The color system is extremely constrained: one brand gold (#C8A020 family), near-black text (#1E1E1A), warm off-white background (#F8F8F5), and one medium grey (#6A6A62).
A small circled-V logomark bug appears bottom-right on every non-cover content slide.
There is exactly one chrome mode: light/warm field; no dark mode is permitted.
Per-slide attention_flow entries describe eye-paths, focal nodes, and scan resets.
The file contains no code symbols; it is pure design documentation.
standouts: Slide titles p02–p17 compose one unbroken spoken pitch sentence via connectives.
Strict single-accent palette and single light-field chrome mode throughout.
Semantic geometry: hexagon = system, octagon = Virtual Tours product node.
Subliminal blueprint texture used as a global background substrate.
Generator-rule style: every design choice is encoded as a reproducible instruction.
conventions: Follows a rigid taxonomy: category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent, generator_rule.
Uses JSON arrays/objects rather than prose or Markdown for the design spec.
Labels product names in body copy as bold near-black, while section heads are gold — a two-register vocabulary-marking convention.
Breaks typical design documentation by including pixel-level approximations (sizes, opacities, hex codes) without image assets or code.
Distinguishes emergent patterns from intentional ones with an explicit boolean.
concerns: The file is truncated mid-object in the 'zone' category ('Title zone — top of every content slide' cuts off after 'recipe':).
Color, opacity, and spacing values are approximate ('approximately', 'or similar') rather than exact tokens.
Evidence claims such as 'All slides' or 'consistent on every content slide' are asserted without attached image samples or measurements.
No slide dimensions, aspect ratio, or asset references are provided.
No versioning, authoring date, or source-media provenance is recorded.
notes: A dense, rule-encoded visual audit of a 17-slide pitch deck that tries to turn every design decision into a reproducible generator rule, but it ends mid-sentence in the zone section.
questions: Were the color/spacing values extracted automatically from images, or hand-estimated?
What is the intended consumer of these generator rules — a layout engine, a design tool, or a human designer?
How should the per-slide attention_flow be validated or used downstream?
What are the exact slide dimensions and aspect ratio?
Is there a companion file with image assets, SVG paths, or the remaining truncated zone content?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a long-form, low-compression 'master grammar source' for the recent-pitches deck. The actual content is a detailed visual-design audit/specification with narrative and attention-flow annotations, which matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: Product-term marking through bold black, not color
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to transform a static pitch deck into a machine-readable generative design system, so the same visual language can be extended, reproduced, or audited programmatically.
novelty: The slide-title sequence is treated as a single continuous spoken sentence; shapes are assigned product-specific semantics; and the document encodes design choices as evidence-tagged generator rules with emergent/intentional classification.
contribution: A granular, taxonomy-driven design grammar that captures per-slide attention flow, shape semantics, color tokens, spacing rules, and generator instructions for a pitch-deck visual system — useful as a substrate for layout generation or design-system fusion.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON design-substrate ledger documenting the narrative arc, per-slide attention flow, and detailed visual-design findings with generator rules for a 17-slide ConceptV investor pitch deck.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a long-form, low-compression master grammar source that can reproduce and extend the deck's visual language consistently.
